blocks.text: watched Nadia go. She walked toward the south end of the beach, away from the house. She didn't look back.
watched Cade lead Marcus toward the far end of the porch. Whatever this was, Marcus hadn't expected it.
went back inside with Mara. Whatever was on the porch could wait.
came back in without having tracked anything in particular.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: You two eat with us tonight. I'm not taking no.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We weren't going to say no.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: Good. Marcus is cooking. Which means Cade will cook half of it and Marcus will take credit.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: He does the same thing with dessert.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I have good taste. That's different from taking credit.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: He pointed at mine and said that one. Like he'd made a decision.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I did make a decision. A very good one.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: One hour. Somebody make sure the table's set.

blocks.prompt: This is the last easy hour of the weekend. Do you feel it?

options.label: Not yet. It still feels like a good weekend.
Something about the afternoon settled wrong.
